risk_if_not_implemented: Without Technical Debt Reviews, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Secure Engineering Principles
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of industry-recognized security, compliance and resilience practices in the specification, design, development, implementation and modification of Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS).
compensating_control_1.justification: Secure Engineering Principles (SEA-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Technical Debt Reviews (SEA-02.3) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to develop an enterprise architecture, aligned with industry-recognized leading practices, with consideration for security, compliance and resilience principles that addresses risk to organizational operations, assets, individuals and other organizations.
compensating_control_2.justification: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture (SEA-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Technical Debt Reviews (SEA-02.3) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
